title: US-3: View Image Files
description: As a user, I want to click on an image file to see it displayed, so that I can view the image content.
acceptance_criteria:
- Clicking an image file opens detail view
- Image is displayed at appropriate size (max-width: 100%)
- Detail view shows: filename, dimensions, file size, modified date
- Back button returns to directory view
- Supports: PNG, JPEG, GIF, SVG, WebP

title: US-1: Browse Directory Structure
description: As a user, I want to see a list of files and folders in a directory, so that I can understand the content organization.
acceptance_criteria:
- Directory view shows all files and subdirectories
- Files and folders are visually distinct (icons, styling)
- File entries show: name, type icon, file size
- Folder entries show: name, folder icon
- Entries are sorted (folders first, then alphabetically)

title: US-4: View Markdown Files
description: As a user, I want to click on a markdown file to see it rendered as HTML, so that I can read documentation and text content.
acceptance_criteria:
- Clicking a .md file opens detail view
- Markdown is rendered as formatted HTML
- Detail view shows: filename, file size, modified date
- Rendered HTML uses readable typography
- Code blocks have syntax highlighting
- Links in markdown are functional

title: Implement markdown pairing logic
description: Add logic to detect and pair .md files with their corresponding files (e.g., logo.png and logo.png.md).
acceptance_criteria:
- Detects paired markdown files
- Excludes paired .md from directory listings
- Includes markdown content in paired file's JSON
- Parses markdown to HTML
- Handles standalone markdown files correctly

title: US-7: Responsive Mobile Experience
description: As a mobile user, I want to browse files on my phone or tablet, so that I can access content from any device.
acceptance_criteria:
- Layout adapts to mobile screen sizes
- Touch interactions work smoothly
- Text is readable without zooming
- Images scale appropriately
- Navigation is thumb-friendly

title: Implement file detail rendering
description: Create the detail view rendering logic for images and markdown files.
acceptance_criteria:
- Renders image files with metadata
- Renders markdown files as HTML
- Renders paired image + markdown
- Shows file metadata (size, date, dimensions)
- Back button returns to browser view
- Handles loading and error states

title: US-2: Navigate Between Directories
description: As a user, I want to click on a folder to view its contents, so that I can explore nested directory structures.
acceptance_criteria:
- Clicking a folder navigates to that folder's view
- Browser URL updates with hash (e.g., #/images/logos)
- Browser back/forward buttons work correctly
- Breadcrumb trail shows current path
- Navigation is instant (no loading delays)

title: Create index.html with js-fileexplorer
description: Build the main HTML page that hosts the file browser and detail view.
acceptance_criteria:
- index.html created in src/
- Includes js-fileexplorer CSS and JS
- Has browser-view and detail-view containers
- Links to app.js and styles.css
- HTML structure is semantic and accessible

title: Setup project structure and dependencies
description: Set up the basic project structure with directories and install/download js-fileexplorer library.
acceptance_criteria:
- Directory structure created (content/, data/, public/, src/, lib/, build/)
- js-fileexplorer downloaded and placed in lib/
- package.json initialized
- Sample content directory created with test files

title: Style the interface with Tailwind CSS
description: Apply Tailwind CSS classes for responsive design and polish.
acceptance_criteria:
- Tailwind CSS integrated (CDN or build process)
- Responsive layout for mobile and desktop
- Beautiful typography with Tailwind classes
- Smooth transitions between views
- File/folder icons and visual distinction
- Loading states styled
- Error messages styled
- Works on mobile devices

title: Implement build script core
description: Create the Node.js build script that walks the content directory and generates the data structure.
acceptance_criteria:
- build/generate.js created
- Recursively walks content/ directory
- Detects files and directories
- Generates unique IDs for each entry
- Creates data/ directory structure

title: US-5: View Image with Markdown Description
description: As a user, I want to see a markdown description alongside an image, so that I can understand the context and details of the image.
acceptance_criteria:
- If file.ext has a paired file.ext.md, the markdown is shown
- Image displays at top of detail view
- Markdown description renders below the image
- The .md file does NOT appear as separate entry in browser
- Works for all supported image formats

title: Implement image thumbnail generation
description: Generate thumbnails for images at build time for use in the file browser.
acceptance_criteria:
- Generates thumbnails for PNG, JPEG, GIF, WebP
- Thumbnails saved to thumbnails/ directory
- Max size: 200x200px
- Preserves aspect ratio
- Extracts image metadata (width, height)

title: Implement router and FileExplorer integration
description: Create app.js with hash-based routing and js-fileexplorer initialization.
acceptance_criteria:
- Hash routing implemented
- FileExplorer initialized with config
- onrefresh callback loads static JSON
- onopenfile callback shows detail view
- onopenitem callback handles folder navigation
- URL updates on navigation

title: US-6: Inline Detail View
description: As a user, I want to see file details without leaving the page, so that I have a seamless browsing experience.
acceptance_criteria:
- Clicking a file toggles from browser to detail view
- Detail view and browser view are part of same page
- Transition is smooth (CSS animations)
- URL hash updates to reflect current file
- Browser back button returns to directory view

title: US-8: Build and Deploy
description: As a content maintainer, I want to run a build script to generate the static site, so that I can deploy updated content.
acceptance_criteria:
- Single command generates all necessary files
- Build script processes content/ directory
- Generates JSON for each directory
- Generates JSON for each file
- Creates thumbnails for images
- Copies original files to public directory
- Build is idempotent (safe to re-run)
- Build script logs progress and errors
